diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thank you for calling Genshin Impact Player Support, this is Chris. How can I help you today?
[Caller] Hi. The email on my account is an old one I don't have access to anymore. I need to change it but every recovery asks me to verify through that email.
[Agent] That's a really common chicken-and-egg problem, and we absolutely have a path that doesn't require the old inbox. Let me pull your account first — UID please?
[Caller] 603245891.
[Agent] Thanks, Marcus. I see your account is linked to PSN, which gives us a great alternate verification route. Because PSN is an authenticated identity provider, we can verify ownership through it instead of the stale email. Sound good?
[Caller] Yes, much better.
[Agent] I'm going to send a verification request to the PSN account tied to this UID. You'll see a prompt on your PlayStation or PlayStation app asking to approve a Genshin Impact account change. Approve it and tell me when you see the confirmation.
[Caller] OK... got it, approved.
[Agent] Verified. Now I also want one more factor for a change this sensitive. Can you tell me the approximate month and year your account was created, and the name of the most recent 5-star character you pulled?
[Caller] Created around late 2021, and the last 5-star was Neuvillette.
[Agent] Both match what I see. Perfect. What's the new email you want on file?
[Caller] marcus.chen.new at email dot com.
[Agent] Updating now. To keep things secure, I've triggered a 24-hour cool-down where the old email is also notified — standard practice so that if this were ever an attempted takeover, the real owner sees it. Since you are the real owner, you can safely ignore any alerts. After 24 hours the old email is fully removed.
[Caller] Makes sense, I appreciate the extra care.
[Agent] Also sending a confirmation to your new email now. Can you confirm receipt?
[Caller] Yes, got it.
[Agent] Excellent. While we're here, I'd strongly recommend enabling 2FA with the new email so this never happens again. I'll include a setup link in the confirmation. Anything else?
[Caller] No, that was exactly what I needed. Thanks Chris.
[Agent] You're very welcome, Marcus. Take care!
